content: By 2025, the incremental prediction error that drives ICM curiosity in autonomous agents can be repurposed to structure an agent’s long-term active memory, where unexpected prediction mismatches not only trigger exploration but also flag new memory modules to be encoded and retained, replacing generic memory pruning rules with curiosity-aligned memory organization. This creates a self-reinforcing loop where an agent’s exploratory drive directly shapes what it remembers for future learning.
